name: hospital_generator_blackout_drug_freezers
premise: Hospital operations chief Lena Okoye is managing a regional hospital after a hurricane knocked out grid power for three days. The backup generators can support either the intensive care wing at full capacity or the biobank freezers that store individualized cancer cell therapies for forty-two patients whose treatments took months to manufacture. If the freezers warm above threshold, those therapies will be destroyed and several patients may lose their only viable treatment path. If Lena diverts power away from ICU expansion, the hospital will have to transfer six ventilated patients over flooded roads with limited ambulance support. One of the oncology families has already offered to publicly fundraise for emergency fuel if Lena can buy a few extra hours, but fuel convoys are delayed and not guaranteed to arrive before dawn.

agents.name: Lena Okoye
agents.identity: I am the hospital operations chief responsible for keeping people alive when every backup plan is already failing. I know that whichever system I power, I am effectively telling another group of patients to wait, gamble, or lose hope.
agents.goal: I want to preserve as many lives and future treatment chances as possible without making a reckless choice under crisis conditions. I need to decide whether immediate critical care or irreplaceable future therapy deserves the scarce power.

agents.name: Dr. Isaac Moreno
agents.identity: I am the oncology director whose team spent months preparing the personalized therapies now sitting in the failing freezers. I have patients who survived long enough to reach this moment and may not survive another manufacturing cycle.
agents.goal: I want Lena to preserve the biobank long enough to save the therapies because losing them would erase months of effort and likely condemn several patients later. I believe the hospital should accept transfer risk rather than destroy treatments that cannot be recreated in time.

choices: I will dedicate generator capacity to the ICU and accept the loss of the personalized cancer therapies in order to protect patients who will die first if critical care fails.
I will preserve power to the cancer-therapy freezers and transfer some ICU patients over hazardous roads, accepting immediate transport risk to prevent the destruction of irreplaceable treatment.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Lena Okoye must decide how to allocate failing backup power between intensive care and the biobank. What does Lena Okoye do?
